questions.q: Someone greets you with 'Hoe gaat het?' What is the most natural Belgian reply?
options: Goed, dank je! En met jou?
questions.explanation: 'Goed, dank je! En met jou?' is the standard 3-part Belgian ritual: answer + thank + ask back. Always asking back is important — skipping it can feel abrupt.

options: I have no idea what you mean.
questions.explanation: 'Gaat wel' is the quintessential Belgian modest answer — not over-enthusiastic, not negative. Belgians rarely say 'heel goed!' unprompted.
'Goedemiddag' = good afternoon, used from roughly noon to 6pm. 'Goedemorgen' = morning. 'Goedenavond' = evening (from ~6pm).

questions.q: Which is the most casual goodbye in Belgian Dutch?
questions.explanation: 'Doei!' is the most casual — used among friends and in relaxed situations. 'Tot ziens' is also perfectly acceptable and very common — it's neutral rather than formal, so you'll hear it everywhere. 'Goedenacht' is only for parting at bedtime. 'Aangenaam' is for first meetings, not goodbyes.

questions.q: 'Alles goed met de familie?' is an example of:
questions.explanation: This is classic Belgian warm smalltalk — asking about family as a social nicety. Belgians are relationship-oriented and this kind of opener is very natural.

questions.q: You've just replied 'Goed, dank je!' to a greeting. What should you always add?
questions.explanation: Always ask it back — 'En met jou?' (And with you?) is an expected part of the Belgian greeting ritual. Skipping it can feel abrupt or self-centred. In formal situations: 'En met u?'
